title: The One with Chandler's Dad

jokes.text: Ross drives it? When I ask, you say you're the only one allowed to.
jokes.setup: Monica claims only she can drive the Porsche
jokes.punchline: But she lets Ross drive it, exposing her inconsistency
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Monica's double standards and favoritism toward Ross

jokes.text: He's my brother, plus he drives so slow he couldn't hurt it.
jokes.setup: Monica needs to justify letting Ross drive
jokes.punchline: Ross drives comically slow - reveals his cautious personality
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail that's both funny and perfectly fits Ross's cautious nature

jokes.text: It's a car, Monica, not a rocket ship. Just replace the bulbs in the brake lights after you're done.
jokes.setup: Ross claims Monica treats the car too preciously
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives fussy maintenance instructions, proving her point
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic reversal - Ross claims Monica's being precious then immediately proves he's just as bad

jokes.text: You let Joey drive it? - I've never driven it. Not once.
jokes.setup: Rachel accuses Monica of letting Joey drive
jokes.punchline: Joey's immediate denial sounds defensive
jokes.explanation: Sets up the escalation but not particularly strong on its own

jokes.text: Okay, once. Okay, I drive it all the time.
jokes.setup: Joey claims he never drives it
jokes.punchline: Immediately escalates through each level of truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure with Joey's characteristic inability to lie convincingly

jokes.text: So much for the 'you-can-drive-it but-don't-tell-Rachel' plan.
jokes.setup: Joey just confessed everything
jokes.punchline: Monica's deadpan acknowledgment that their secret plan failed spectacularly
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction that acknowledges the failure of their scheme

jokes.text: I can fix this. Monica, Rachel thinks all you talk about is the wedding.
jokes.setup: Monica claims she can fix the Porsche situation
jokes.punchline: Her 'fix' is completely changing the subject to something worse
jokes.explanation: Monica's panicked logic of 'fixing' one problem by creating a different one

jokes.text: I won't let you drive the Porsche because you're a terrible driver.
jokes.setup: Monica tries to prove she doesn't only talk about weddings
jokes.punchline: Bluntly insults Rachel instead
jokes.explanation: Monica's characteristically blunt honesty delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: There. That wasn't about the wedding.
jokes.setup: Monica just insulted Rachel
jokes.punchline: Proudly notes she technically avoided wedding talk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica logic - technically correct while missing the actual problem

jokes.text: You got a couple hours?
jokes.setup: Monica offers to take Rachel around the block
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically implies Monica drives so slow it would take hours
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the earlier slow driving joke, applied to Monica

jokes.text: Whew, that was a brisk ride. - Take the top down, did you? - Only way to fly.
jokes.setup: Joey acts like they had an exciting ride
jokes.punchline: Visual reveal that his hair is wildly messed up from wind
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag with hair blown by wind, competently executed

jokes.text: In high school, that added up to head cheerleader.
jokes.setup: Ross says fast and irresponsible makes a bad driver
jokes.punchline: Rachel reveals those same traits made her head cheerleader
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion showing how the same traits can be positive in different contexts

jokes.text: Did you see how she looked at me? She must've seen me cruising in the bad boy. - She's checking out your beehive. - What?
jokes.setup: Joey thinks a woman was attracted to him and the car
jokes.punchline: She was actually staring at his ridiculous wind-blown hair
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke playing off the previous visual gag

jokes.text: Give me a brush. - The keys. - No way! - Well, no brush. Fine. It doesn't matter. If I remember correctly, there's a comb on the floor of the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Joey needs to fix his hair but Ross wants the keys
jokes.punchline: Joey's increasingly desperate solutions ending with a gross bathroom floor comb
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's vanity and willingness to use unsanitary solutions

jokes.text: Alimony.
jokes.setup: Context suggests someone asked what Chandler is reading/doing
jokes.punchline: Single word answer implies he's dealing with divorce paperwork
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word delivers a complete dark joke about his parents' relationship

jokes.text: Oh, right. Maybe that's because I didn't send him an invitation.
jokes.setup: Monica wonders why they haven't heard from Chandler's dad
jokes.punchline: Chandler reveals he didn't invite his own father
jokes.explanation: Chandler's characteristic way of dropping uncomfortable family information casually

jokes.text: We don't run in the same circles. I hang out with you guys and he... stars in a drag show in Vegas.
jokes.setup: Chandler says they don't run in the same circles
jokes.punchline: Reveals his dad performs drag in Vegas - explains the disconnect
jokes.explanation: Great setup/payoff that explains Chandler's discomfort while being genuinely funny

jokes.text: I think I want to trade circles.
jokes.setup: Chandler reveals his dad's drag career
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately wants to switch social circles
jokes.explanation: Monica's immediate enthusiasm for the more exciting social circle

jokes.text: You don't want him there. Nobody will stare at the bride when the groom's father's wearing a backless dress.
jokes.setup: Monica wants his dad at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Chandler paints a vivid picture of his drag queen father upstaging the bride
jokes.explanation: Vivid visual imagery that's both funny and shows Chandler's genuine concern

jokes.text: As long as he's not wearing a white dress and veil, I don't care.
jokes.setup: Chandler worries his dad will upstage Monica
jokes.punchline: Monica only cares about protecting her specific bridal elements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica response - practical and wedding-focused even about drag queens

jokes.text: I think I need to do some shopping.
jokes.setup: Monica says she doesn't care what his dad wears as long as it's not white
jokes.punchline: Chandler implies he might need to buy his dad a white dress to keep him away
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Chandler would sabotage his own wedding to avoid his dad

jokes.text: There's no way I'll let you drive this car. Just hand over the keys. Oh! Do not start this car.
jokes.setup: Monica tries to prevent Rachel from driving
jokes.punchline: Physical struggle implied, with Monica's panic escalating
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup, functional but not particularly inspired

jokes.text: Okay, okay. I will give you 20 bucks if you get out of this car right now. Hey!
jokes.setup: Monica is desperate to get Rachel out of the car
jokes.punchline: Tries to bribe her, showing how desperate she's become
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's control issues escalating to bribery

jokes.text: If you're so freaked out, get in the car. With you? Yeah, right. All right. Okay, okay, okay.
jokes.setup: Rachel challenges Monica to get in if she's so worried
jokes.punchline: Monica's terror is stronger than her logical response
jokes.explanation: Monica's fear overrides her logic - good character consistency

jokes.text: What are you doing? Get in front. - In the death seat?!
jokes.setup: Rachel tells Monica to get in the front seat
jokes.punchline: Monica calls it the 'death seat' showing her terror of Rachel's driving
jokes.explanation: Good exaggeration showing Monica's genuine fear of Rachel's driving skills

jokes.text: That one kept going.
jokes.setup: Context implies someone's kiss went on too long
jokes.punchline: Ross's understated observation about an overly long kiss
jokes.explanation: Ross's characteristic awkward observation of romantic situations

jokes.text: What's your name?
jokes.setup: Ross wants to get to know Phoebe's boyfriend better
jokes.punchline: Reveals he doesn't even know the guy's name after a month
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward Ross moment - wants to bond but has missed basic details

jokes.text: Great! Let me make sure I'm not doing anything Tuesday.
jokes.setup: Ross invites Jake to a Knicks game Tuesday
jokes.punchline: Jake needs to check his schedule, implying he might be busy with Phoebe
jokes.explanation: Mild awkwardness but not particularly strong comedically

jokes.text: We're not eloping. Can our wedding be bigger, please?
jokes.setup: Chandler suggests eloping to avoid wedding stress
jokes.punchline: Monica wants the wedding to be even bigger instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica response - completely opposite of what Chandler wants

jokes.text: Now that you're marrying me, you don't get to win anymore.
jokes.setup: Chandler claims he won their argument about Vegas
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals marriage means Chandler loses all future arguments
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny take on marriage dynamics, perfectly in character for Monica

jokes.text: You need a new word for what I went through.
jokes.setup: Monica says all kids are embarrassed by their parents
jokes.punchline: Chandler implies his embarrassment was beyond normal levels
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the story to come, shows the magnitude of Chandler's issues

jokes.text: In high school, he came to all my swim meets... dressed as a Hollywood starlet.
jokes.setup: Chandler begins explaining his embarrassment
jokes.punchline: His dad attended swim meets in drag as famous actresses
jokes.explanation: Establishes the specific embarrassing scenario with good visual detail

jokes.text: Hard enough to be 14, skinny, wearing Speedos Mom promised I'd grow into.
jokes.setup: Chandler describes being 14 at swim meets
jokes.punchline: Paints picture of awkward teen in oversized swimwear
jokes.explanation: Vivid self-deprecating image that builds empathy while being funny

jokes.text: Dressed as Carmen Miranda, wearing a headdress with real fruit... that he'll later give to your friends as a healthy snack.
jokes.setup: Chandler's dad dressed as Hollywood starlets
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd details including fruit costume becoming snacks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with absurd but believable details. The fruit-as-snacks detail is inspired writing.

jokes.text: He was at every one of your swim meets cheering you on. That's a pretty great dad.
jokes.setup: Chandler describes his embarrassing father
jokes.punchline: Monica reframes it as devoted parenting
jokes.explanation: Smart perspective shift that reframes the embarrassment as love

jokes.text: He had sex with Mr. Garibaldi. - Who's Mr. Garibaldi? - Does it matter?!
jokes.setup: Monica suggests his dad was great
jokes.punchline: Chandler drops another bombshell, then his exasperation at having to explain
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with Chandler's exasperated 'Does it matter?!' being perfectly in character

jokes.text: How much did you ever win before?
jokes.setup: Monica says he won't win arguments anymore
jokes.punchline: Chandler admits he never won before either
jokes.explanation: Self-aware callback that shows Chandler's realistic self-assessment

jokes.text: You know how when you're wearing pants and you lean forward... I check out your underwear? Well, when Jake did it... I saw that he was wearing women's underwear.
jokes.setup: Ross starts with TMI about checking out underwear
jokes.punchline: Reveals Jake wears women's underwear
jokes.explanation: Awkward Ross oversharing that leads to the episode's subplot

jokes.text: I know. They were mine.
jokes.setup: Ross is scandalized about Jake wearing women's underwear
jokes.punchline: Phoebe casually admits they're her underwear
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe response - completely defuses Ross's concern with casual acceptance

jokes.text: We were goofing around, and I dared him to try them on.
jokes.setup: Everyone reacts to the underwear revelation
jokes.punchline: Phoebe explains it as playful couple behavior
jokes.explanation: Typical Phoebe - makes unusual relationship behavior seem normal

jokes.text: I'm wearing his briefs right now. - That's kind of hot.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explains the underwear sharing
jokes.punchline: Reveals it goes both ways, Monica finds it attractive
jokes.explanation: Shows the mutual nature of their underwear sharing and Monica's reaction

jokes.text: I think so too. And that little flap? Great for holding my lipstick.
jokes.setup: Monica finds the underwear sharing hot
jokes.punchline: Phoebe reveals she uses men's underwear features for feminine purposes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe logic - finding practical feminine uses for masculine underwear design

jokes.text: Next he'll say your high heels are good for his posture.
jokes.setup: Phoebe defends Jake's comfort in women's underwear
jokes.punchline: Ross escalates to an even more feminine clothing item
jokes.explanation: Ross's discomfort escalating to more extreme examples

jokes.text: There's nothing wrong with Jake. He is all man. I think more than you.
jokes.setup: Ross questions Jake's masculinity
jokes.punchline: Phoebe turns it around to question Ross's masculinity
jokes.explanation: Phoebe defending her man by attacking Ross - good character dynamics

jokes.text: He looked like a real lumberjack in those pink lacies.
jokes.setup: Phoebe claims Jake is very masculine
jokes.punchline: The image of a lumberjack in pink lace underwear
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd juxtaposition of hypermasculine lumberjack with delicate lingerie

jokes.text: Only a man completely secure in his masculinity could... walk around in women's underwear. I don't think you could.
jokes.setup: Discussing masculine security
jokes.punchline: Challenges Ross to prove his masculinity by wearing women's underwear
jokes.explanation: Clever psychological manipulation - using masculinity challenge to get Ross to cross-dress

jokes.text: You've seen my huge stack of porn.
jokes.setup: Ross needs to prove his masculinity
jokes.punchline: Points to his porn collection as evidence
jokes.explanation: Ross's awkward overshare trying to prove his heterosexuality

jokes.text: You don't have a valid driver's license? Pull over right now.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentions needing to renew her license
jokes.punchline: Monica realizes Rachel is driving illegally and panics
jokes.explanation: Monica's control freak nature escalating when she realizes the legal issue

jokes.text: I am not horsing around, okay? I am 'Porsche-ing' around.
jokes.setup: Monica accuses Rachel of horsing around while driving
jokes.punchline: Rachel makes a pun with the car brand
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that fits the situation, though somewhat forced

jokes.text: Stay calm. Nothing will happen. You're not in that much trouble. Really? You think so? I'm talking to myself. You're going down!
jokes.setup: Monica seems to be calming Rachel down
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was talking to herself, then threatens Rachel
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Monica's internal panic vs external threats

jokes.text: I just ordered a beer. - You're straight. I get it.
jokes.setup: Chandler has trouble with pronouns for the drag queen waitress
jokes.punchline: Waitress assumes he's uncomfortable because he's straight
jokes.explanation: Good reversal - the drag queen is understanding rather than offended

jokes.text: It's very 'Cats in the Cradle.' I don't want to get into it.
jokes.setup: Chandler explains avoiding his father's attempts to reconnect
jokes.punchline: References the famous song about father-son relationships
jokes.explanation: Smart cultural reference that perfectly captures Chandler's situation

jokes.text: And there's Daddy.
jokes.setup: Helena Handbasket is introduced on stage
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mortified recognition of his father
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character reaction to seeing his drag queen father perform

jokes.text: It's weird. I had a dream last night that I was stopped by a policeman. And then he... Well, I probably shouldn't tell you the rest.
jokes.setup: Rachel gets pulled over and tries to flirt
jokes.punchline: Implies a sexual dream about police officers
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo joke, competent but predictable

jokes.text: Officer... Handsome. - It's Hanson. - Oops, sorry. My mistake.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries to flirt by calling the officer handsome
jokes.punchline: Misreads his name tag, creating awkward correction
jokes.explanation: Standard flirtation-gone-wrong joke with mild embarrassment

jokes.text: Really? You think so? You know, I had just rolled right out of bed.
jokes.setup: Officer compliments her license photo
jokes.punchline: Rachel pretends it's a natural, no-effort look
jokes.explanation: Rachel's vanity showing as she takes credit for a professional photo

jokes.text: She should! It was taken 10 years ago!
jokes.setup: Rachel brags about her natural beauty in the photo
jokes.punchline: Monica exposes that it's a decade old
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica undermining Rachel's vanity with factual brutality

jokes.text: You're an Aquarius, huh? - I'll bet you're a Gemini. - No. - Taurus? - No. - Virgo? - No. - Sagittarius? - Yep. I knew it. I knew it!
jokes.setup: Officer tries to guess Rachel's astrological sign
jokes.punchline: Goes through multiple wrong guesses before getting it right and acting like he knew all along
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the officer's fake psychic confidence

jokes.text: Can he handle a stick? - Well... - I can handle a stick!
jokes.setup: Officer asks about manual transmission ability
jokes.punchline: Monica enthusiastically volunteers, missing possible innuendo
jokes.explanation: Double entendre with Monica's innocent enthusiasm

jokes.text: That's Mr. Garibaldi playing the piano.
jokes.setup: Chandler spots someone at the piano
jokes.punchline: Reveals this is the mysterious Mr. Garibaldi from his earlier story
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that connects earlier exposition to visual payoff

jokes.text: Oh, much better. You're invisible now.
jokes.setup: Chandler tries to hide by putting a napkin over his face
jokes.punchline: Monica sarcastically notes his failed camouflage attempt
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response to Chandler's childish hiding attempt

jokes.text: Where are you from? - Bakersfield. - I'm sorry? - Bakersfield. No, no, I heard. I'm just sorry.
jokes.setup: Helena asks where someone is from
jokes.punchline: Pretends not to hear 'Bakersfield' but clarifies she heard, just feels bad for them
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structure with the fake mishearing leading to the insult

jokes.text: Look, a standing ovation already. So early in the show.
jokes.setup: Chandler stands up to leave
jokes.punchline: Helena pretends he's giving a standing ovation
jokes.explanation: Smart performer move turning an audience member's exit into part of the show

jokes.text: Can we have our drinks, please? Waiter? ...tress?
jokes.setup: Chandler tries to order from a drag queen server
jokes.punchline: Struggles with the correct pronoun, creating awkward pause
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Chandler's discomfort with his father's world

jokes.text: How much of a man am I? Wow, nice. Manly, and also kind of a slut.
jokes.setup: Ross shows up wearing women's underwear
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's assessment combines masculinity with promiscuity
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's unexpected combination of compliment and backhanded insult

jokes.text: The silk feels really good. And things aren't as smashed down as I thought they would be.
jokes.setup: Ross admits to enjoying women's underwear
jokes.punchline: Gives detailed review of the comfort and fit
jokes.explanation: Ross's analytical approach to cross-dressing creates uncomfortable humor

jokes.text: You have so many more choices than with men's underwear. Bikini, French cut, thong. And the fabrics: cotton, silk, lace.
jokes.setup: Ross is getting into wearing women's underwear
jokes.punchline: Becomes an expert on women's underwear varieties
jokes.explanation: Ross's characteristic over-analysis applied to lingerie shopping

jokes.text: What's your name? Chandler. Chandler? What an unusual name. You must have had terribly fascinating parents. Oh, they're a hoot.
jokes.setup: Helena comments on Chandler's unusual name and parents
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically agrees they're 'a hoot' while talking to his drag queen father
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - audience knows Helena is his father making the comment

jokes.text: I'm not very fond of New York. Queens, I like.
jokes.setup: Helena doesn't like New York
jokes.punchline: Likes Queens - both the borough and a drag reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double meaning working on both literal and drag culture levels

jokes.text: So, you're bald.
jokes.setup: Helena is having a nice conversation with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Abruptly and bluntly comments on his baldness
jokes.explanation: Perfect abrupt topic change that creates uncomfortable humor

jokes.text: Then I wouldn't miss it for the world.
jokes.setup: Chandler invites his father to the wedding
jokes.punchline: Helena immediately and enthusiastically accepts
jokes.explanation: Touching moment that shows Helena's genuine love for Chandler

jokes.text: I'm getting all misty here. You'd think I was having my legs waxed or something.
jokes.setup: Helena is emotional about the wedding invitation
jokes.punchline: Compares tears to a painful beauty treatment
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific reference that maintains the drag queen persona while showing emotion

jokes.text: When I was growing up, I played the one on the far left.
jokes.setup: Context suggests performers are on stage
jokes.punchline: Helena reveals childhood connection to the performance
jokes.explanation: Nice character background detail that explains Helena's performance background

jokes.text: Remind me to introduce you to someone. - Who? - Fourth gear.
jokes.setup: Ross mysteriously mentions introducing Rachel to someone
jokes.punchline: Reveals he means a gear in the transmission
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke about Rachel's driving and gear usage

jokes.text: Maybe he saw your hands slip briefly from the 10 and 2:00 position.
jokes.setup: They wonder why they're being pulled over again
jokes.punchline: Ross suggests a minor driving school violation
jokes.explanation: Ross's nerdy attention to driving school rules

jokes.text: Maybe it's Sergeant Sagittarius coming back to flirt some more.
jokes.setup: Another police stop
jokes.punchline: Ross references the earlier astrological flirtation
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to the earlier scene with the astrology-loving officer

jokes.text: You're right. It was 37.
jokes.setup: Ross guesses they weren't going more than 60
jokes.punchline: Reveals they were going 37 - too slow, not too fast
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - they're getting a ticket for driving too slowly, not too fast

jokes.text: You're not ticketing me for driving too slow, are you?
jokes.setup: Ross realizes they were going 37 mph
jokes.punchline: Asks the obvious question with dawning horror
jokes.explanation: Ross's mortification at being ticketed for his characteristic slow driving

jokes.text: You know, officer... I had the weirdest dream last night. Oh, my God! Your license, please.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries the same flirtation technique
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's a different officer and panics
jokes.explanation: Rachel tries to repeat her earlier 'success' but with wrong officer

jokes.text: Officer... Pretty? - It's Petty.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries the same name-calling flirtation
jokes.punchline: Another name correction, this time less flattering
jokes.explanation: Repeats the name-misreading gag with less success

jokes.text: Not that I'm insecure about my manhood or anything, but... I need to hook up with a woman right now.
jokes.setup: Ross claims he's not insecure about masculinity
jokes.punchline: Immediately needs to prove his heterosexuality after wearing lingerie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross psychology - denying insecurity while proving it

jokes.text: You look familiar. Do I know you? I don't think so. Maybe it's because I'm on television. I'm an actor on Days of our Lives.
jokes.setup: Woman thinks Joey looks familiar
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately brags about being on TV
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey vanity - assumes recognition must be from his TV work

tmdb_overview: Monica's Porsche reappears; Ross and Rachel take an adventurous drive. Chandler and Monica visit Vegas seeking Chandler's estranged father. Joey proves masculinity to Phoebe.
